notes.note: "Gladiator" was a standard term of abuse in Cicero's invective against Antony, casting him as a brutal, low hireling; the war is the political struggle of late 44 BC, fought "words against arms" (Cicero's speeches against Antony's troops).
Antony was Cicero's colleague in the college of augurs (conlega), a priestly tie that sharpens the insult of calling him a gladiator.

notes.anchor: our tyrant-slayers are at the world's end
notes.note: The tyrannoctoni are the assassins of Caesar (chiefly M. Brutus and Cassius), who by late 44 BC had withdrawn to the eastern provinces, leaving the loyalists in Rome leaderless.
